faqs.q: What is Internal Family Systems therapy?
faqs.a: IFS is a form of psychotherapy built on the idea that the mind is naturally made up of many parts, each with its own perspective and job. It also describes a calm, curious, compassionate core Self that the person learns to lead from.

faqs.q: Does having parts in IFS mean I have a dissociative disorder?
faqs.a: No. IFS isn't a sign that someone has multiple personalities or a dissociative disorder. Having parts is described as a normal feature of every mind, not a symptom.

faqs.q: Is the goal of IFS to get rid of unwanted parts?
faqs.a: No. The goal is to understand and unburden parts, not to wage war on the parts of yourself you don't like. As parts are met with calm understanding, they tend to relax their extreme roles.
